questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.1, what is the primary objective of monitoring and measurement activities within an Information Security Management System?
options: C. To eliminate all non-conformities before they impact business operations
B. To identify all potential threats and vulnerabilities in the organization's IT infrastructure
D. To ensure that all employees comply with the organization's disciplinary policies
A. To determine the conformity of the ISMS with the requirements and to evaluate the effectiveness of security controls
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.1 explicitly requires organizations to determine conformity and evaluate effectiveness. Monitoring and measurement provide evidence of ISMS performance and control effectiveness, enabling data-driven decision-making and continuous improvement.
questions.justification_wrong: While threat identification and employee compliance are important, they are not the primary objectives of Clause 9.1 monitoring. Complete threat elimination is unrealistic; monitoring focuses on conformity and effectiveness evaluation.

questions.text: In accordance with ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.2, what determines the scope, frequency, and methods for conducting internal audits of the ISMS?
options: A. The external auditor's recommendations and the audit firm's standard procedures
D. The availability of budget and personnel resources allocated by senior management
C. The regulatory requirements of the country where the organization operates
B. The organization's audit plan, which must consider the importance of processes and previous audit results
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.2.1 requires organizations to establish audit criteria and scope based on importance and previous results. The audit plan must be documented and consider organizational context, ensuring systematic evaluation of ISMS effectiveness.
questions.justification_wrong: External auditor recommendations inform improvement but don't determine internal audit scope. Regulatory requirements are inputs, not determinants. Budget availability is a constraint, not a determining factor for audit planning according to normative requirements.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.3, what actions must the organization take when it identifies non-conformities in its ISMS?
options: A. Document the non-conformity and communicate it only to affected parties; corrective actions are optional
D. Report all non-conformities to external regulators within 24 hours without conducting internal investigation
C. Take action to control, correct, and eliminate non-conformities; take actions to deal with consequences and evaluate need for corrective action
B. Immediately terminate all related processes until the non-conformity is completely resolved
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.3 mandates immediate control/correction, consequence management, and evaluation for corrective action necessity. This systematic approach ensures non-conformities are addressed comprehensively, preventing recurrence and managing residual risks effectively.
questions.justification_wrong: Corrective actions are mandatory, not optional. Process termination is disproportionate; containment and correction are required. Automatic regulator notification isn't specified; investigation and evaluation are organizational responsibilities per Clause 9.3 requirements.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 Clause 10.2, what is the key requirement for implementing corrective actions identified during ISMS management review?
options: C. Corrective actions are only necessary if they relate to security incidents that caused financial loss
D. All corrective actions must be implemented within 30 days without exception or documentation
A. Corrective actions must be approved by external auditors before implementation
B. Organizations should document, implement, verify effectiveness, and review outcomes of corrective actions; effectiveness must be evaluated
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 10.2 requires organizations to determine, implement, and evaluate effectiveness of corrective actions. This includes documenting the process and reviewing results, ensuring continuous improvement of the ISMS and prevention of recurrence.
questions.justification_wrong: External auditor approval isn't required for corrective action implementation. Limitation to incidents causing financial loss contradicts scope; Clause 10.2 applies to all management review findings. Fixed timelines without evaluation conflict with systematic effectiveness verification requirements.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 Clause 10.1, what must the organization's management review assess regarding the ISMS?
options: D. The compliance status with international standards and competitor security posture in the industry
A. Only the budget allocated to information security and the number of security incidents reported
C. Whether the organization should replace its Information Security Manager based on performance metrics
B. The extent to which objectives have been achieved, results of monitoring, internal audit findings, and need for changes to the ISMS
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 10.1 specifies management review must assess objective achievement, monitoring results, audit findings, and organizational change needs. This comprehensive evaluation ensures ISMS remains suitable, adequate, and effective in addressing organizational context evolution.
questions.justification_wrong: Budget and incident count alone are insufficient; management review requires broader assessment. Personnel decisions aren't Clause 10.1 scope. Competitor comparison isn't normatively required; focus is internal ISMS suitability and adequacy, not external benchmarking.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.1, what is the primary objective of monitoring and measuring information security performance?
options: A. To evaluate the effectiveness of controls and identify deviations from established objectives
D. To ensure compliance with external regulations without internal assessment
B. To replace internal audits and eliminate the need for management reviews
C. To document all security incidents regardless of their severity level
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.1 requires organizations to determine what needs monitoring, the methods, when results are analyzed, and who analyzes them. This supports evaluating control effectiveness and identifying performance gaps against ISMS objectives.
questions.justification_wrong: Monitoring cannot replace audits (Clause 9.2) or eliminate reviews (Clause 9.3). Incident documentation follows Clause 7.5, not monitoring. External compliance alone is insufficient for ISMS effectiveness.

questions.text: In accordance with Clause 9.2 (Internal Audit), what is a critical requirement regarding the independence and objectivity of internal auditors?
options: C. Internal audits may be conducted by operational staff to reduce costs and increase efficiency
D. The same auditor may audit all areas annually to ensure consistency in assessment criteria
A. Internal auditors must report directly to the CISO and have no other organizational responsibilities
B. Auditors should not audit areas where they have direct responsibility, ensuring objectivity and preventing conflicts of interest
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.2 mandates that internal audits ensure independence and objectivity. Auditors must not audit areas under their direct responsibility to maintain impartiality and credibility of audit results.
questions.justification_wrong: Reporting structure varies by organization. Operational staff lack audit expertise. Auditor rotation prevents bias and ensures fresh perspectives. Independence requires separation from audited functions.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 Clause 10.1 (Nonconformity and Corrective Action), what must an organization do when a nonconformity is detected?
options: D. Establish preventive actions for all potential future nonconformities before any detection occurs
A. Document it, communicate findings, evaluate causes, determine corrective actions, and review effectiveness
C. Report only critical nonconformities to management; minor issues require no formal action
B. Immediately remove the nonconforming asset from production without further investigation
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 10.1 requires organizations to: react to nonconformities, evaluate causes, determine and implement corrective actions, and review effectiveness. Documentation and communication are mandatory requirements.
questions.justification_wrong: Reactive removal bypasses root cause analysis. All nonconformities require formal treatment per Clause 10.1. Preventive actions address risks, not detected nonconformities—these are corrective actions.

questions.text: What is the relationship between Clause 9.3 (Management Review) and the continual improvement process described in Clause 10.3?
options: C. Continual improvement requires only technical changes; management reviews address only compliance issues
A. Management reviews provide input data and decisions that drive the organization's continual improvement initiatives
B. Clause 9.3 and 10.3 are independent processes with no direct connection or interdependencies
D. Management reviews occur annually while continual improvement is a one-time initiative after certification
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 integrates management review (Clause 9.3) findings—including performance data, audit results, nonconformities—as key inputs for continual improvement (Clause 10.3). This creates a systematic enhancement cycle.
questions.justification_wrong: The clauses are interconnected within the PDCA cycle. Improvement encompasses all ISMS aspects. Continual improvement is ongoing; frequency depends on organizational needs, not fixed schedules.

questions.text: In ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.2, what information must an organization document and retain regarding internal audit results?
options: B. Only the names of auditors and dates of audit execution to maintain a simple record
D. Audit results only for nonconformities; conforming areas need not be documented
A. Audit scope, audit criteria, findings, evidence of nonconformities, and recommendations for improvement
C. Summary statistics without detailed findings to protect sensitive internal security information
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.2 requires organizations to retain documented information on audit scope, criteria, findings, evidence of nonconformities, and recommendations. This supports traceability and improvement decisions.
questions.justification_wrong: Auditor names alone lack substance. Summary statistics omit evidence needed for corrective action. Conforming areas must also be documented to demonstrate control effectiveness and provide baseline data.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.2, which element is NOT mandatory when establishing monitoring and measurement procedures for the ISMS?
options: D. Definition of when results shall be analyzed and evaluated
B. Definition of methods for monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
A. Definition of what will be monitored and measured
C. Specification of the exact frequency of third-party audits
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.2 requires defining what, methods, and when to monitor/measure. Third-party audit frequency is not mandated; internal audits are required instead per Clause 9.2.1.
questions.justification_wrong: Clauses 9.2 explicitly requires defining what will be monitored, methods for analysis, and timing. Only internal audits are mandatory; external audits are optional supplementary measures.

questions.text: During an internal audit of the ISMS, an auditor discovers that corrective actions from the previous audit remain incomplete after the deadline. Per ISO 27001:2022 Clause 10.1, what is the organization's obligation?
options: A. Immediately terminate the ISMS until all actions are resolved
B. Determine whether this constitutes a non-conformity and take appropriate corrective actions
C. Report the delay to external auditors only; internal documentation is sufficient
D. Extend the deadline indefinitely and reassess annually
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 10.1 mandates determining non-conformities and their causes, then implementing corrective actions to prevent recurrence. Incomplete actions represent non-conformity requiring proper management.
questions.justification_wrong: Terminating the ISMS is excessive; extending deadlines bypasses accountability. External reporting alone is insufficient; ISO 27001:2022 requires documented analysis and corrective action planning regardless of audit type.

questions.text: An organization conducts its management review per ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.3. Which input is MANDATORY for this review?
options: A. Results from the most recent internal audit and performance of security controls
B. Feedback from external consultants only
D. Marketing and sales performance metrics
C. Annual budget allocations and IT infrastructure costs
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.3 requires management review inputs including internal audit results (9.2.1), monitoring/measurement results, non-conformities, and corrective action status—all directly related to ISMS performance.
questions.justification_wrong: External consultants, budget metrics, and sales data are not specified inputs in Clause 9.3. Management review must focus on ISMS-specific inputs to ensure suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the system.

questions.text: An organization identifies that a security control is no longer effective due to an emerging threat landscape. Per ISO 27001:2022 Clause 10.3, what is the primary requirement?
options: C. Continually improve the suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the ISMS and its controls
B. Maintain the control as-is to avoid disrupting existing processes
D. Wait for the next annual management review to propose changes
A. Immediately purchase new security tools without evaluation
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 10.3 explicitly requires the organization to 'continually improve the suitability, adequacy and effectiveness of the ISMS.' This is an ongoing obligation, not periodic.
questions.justification_wrong: Purchasing without evaluation contradicts the systematic approach. Maintaining ineffective controls violates the continual improvement requirement. Waiting until annual review delays necessary improvements to security posture.

questions.text: During internal audit planning per ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.2.1, which factor should determine audit scope and frequency?
options: B. The organizational budget available for audit activities only
C. External regulatory requirements in competing industries
A. The importance of the processes being audited and previous audit results
D. The personal preferences of the internal audit team
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.2.1 requires internal audit programs considering 'the importance of the processes' and 'results of previous audits.' Risk-based approach determines scope/frequency, not budget or external factors.
questions.justification_wrong: Budget constraints do not justify inadequate audit coverage. Competing industry regulations are irrelevant to the organization's own ISMS. Personal preferences lack objective criteria. Risk and significance of processes must drive audit planning.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.2, which of the following best describes the primary objective of internal audits within an ISMS?
options: D. To perform compliance checks solely against regulatory frameworks without reference to ISMS design
B. To identify all security incidents and assign corrective actions immediately without documented evidence
C. To replace external audits and eliminate the need for third-party certification
A. To determine conformity of the ISMS with ISO 27001 requirements and organizational requirements, and effectiveness of implemented controls
questions.justification_correct: Clause 9.2.1 requires internal audits assess conformity with ISO 27001, organizational requirements, and control effectiveness. This enables identification of non-conformities and drives continual improvement through systematic, documented evaluation.
questions.justification_wrong: Internal audits must follow documented procedures (9.2.2), cannot replace external audits, and must assess ISMS design and operation against all applicable criteria including ISO 27001 normative requirements.

questions.text: During the management review process (ISO 27001:2022, clause 9.3), which input is MOST critical for evaluating ISMS effectiveness and identifying improvement opportunities?
options: C. Anecdotal feedback from end-users without documented evidence or metrics
B. Only financial reports and budget allocations for the next fiscal year
D. Recommendations from competitors' security implementations regardless of organizational context
A. Results of monitoring and measurement activities, internal audit findings, non-conformities, corrective actions, and changes in external/internal context
questions.justification_correct: Clause 9.3.2 specifies management review inputs include monitoring/measurement results, audit findings, non-conformities, corrective actions, and contextual changes. These data-driven inputs enable objective effectiveness evaluation and evidence-based improvement decisions.
questions.justification_wrong: Management review must be based on objective evidence and documented information (clause 4.4.2), not anecdotal data or external benchmarks alone. Financial data alone cannot assess ISMS design and operational effectiveness.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.2, when a non-conformity is identified during monitoring or auditing, what is the mandatory sequence for handling corrective actions?
options: C. Document the issue and defer action until the next scheduled management review meeting
B. Immediately implement the most obvious fix without root cause analysis to minimize downtime
A. Evaluate cause, determine if similar non-conformities exist, take corrective action, verify effectiveness, update documented information
D. Report to external auditors first and wait for their recommendations before acting
questions.justification_correct: Clause 10.2 mandates systematic evaluation of non-conformities, including root cause analysis (10.2.1), assessment of similar issues (10.2.2), corrective action determination (10.2.3), effectiveness verification (10.2.4), and documented information updates (10.2.5).
questions.justification_wrong: Quick fixes without root cause analysis risk recurring non-conformities. Management review cannot replace corrective actions. External auditors evaluate ISMS; internal management must address non-conformities proactively and timely per clause 10.2 requirements.

questions.text: In ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.1, which monitoring and measurement activities must be planned to ensure ongoing ISMS performance assessment?
questions.tag: Monitoring and Measurement - Planning and Execution
options: D. Ad-hoc assessments performed only when security incidents occur
A. Monitoring of ISMS processes, measurement of control effectiveness, analysis of monitoring data, and determination of reporting methods
B. Monthly elimination of all existing controls to test system resilience
C. Reliance solely on user complaints as the primary performance indicator
questions.justification_correct: Clause 9.1.1 requires organizations plan what processes need monitoring, how to measure effectiveness, when analysis occurs, and how results are reported. Clause 9.1.2 mandates documented retention of monitoring/measurement evidence for traceability.
questions.justification_wrong: Eliminating controls contradicts 9.1 intent of ensuring effectiveness. User complaints alone are insufficient; systematic measurement per 9.1.1 requires predetermined criteria. Ad-hoc assessment cannot establish control effectiveness trends required by clause 9.1.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.3, what determines whether continual improvement of the ISMS is effective and aligned with organizational objectives?
options: C. Continuous improvement only when external regulators mandate changes
B. Random implementation of all suggested improvements without prioritization or resource consideration
A. Systematic identification and selection of improvement opportunities, analysis of their priority, implementation based on risk assessment, and documented evidence of outcomes
D. Annual replacement of all security controls with newer technologies regardless of current effectiveness
questions.justification_correct: Clause 10.3 emphasizes organizations must systematically identify improvement opportunities, determine which to implement based on analysis and risk evaluation, and maintain documented information of improvements undertaken, demonstrating evidence-based, objective decision-making.
questions.justification_wrong: Random improvements waste resources and may introduce risks. Improvement must be proactive, not reactive to external mandates. Control replacement decisions require assessment of current effectiveness per clause 9.1, not automatic technology cycling.

questions.text: During an internal audit of the ISMS, the auditor discovers that nonconformities identified in the previous audit cycle were addressed through immediate corrective actions only, without root cause analysis. According to ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.1, what is the primary deficiency in this approach?
options: C. The organization must wait for a second occurrence before performing root cause analysis
B. Immediate corrective actions are sufficient if they resolve the nonconformity within 30 days
D. Corrective actions require management approval before implementation, regardless of root cause
A. Root cause analysis is mandatory to prevent recurrence and address systemic issues, not just symptoms
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.1 requires organizations to determine and implement actions to eliminate causes of nonconformities to prevent recurrence. Root cause analysis is essential for corrective actions to be effective and systematic.
questions.justification_wrong: Immediate actions without root analysis may address symptoms temporarily but do not prevent recurrence, violating the principle of corrective action.

questions.text: An organization implements a KPI dashboard showing security incident metrics updated daily. However, the management review process (clause 9.3) still relies on manual data aggregation quarterly. What is the key issue with this monitoring approach?
options: D. KPI dashboards are not recognized as valid monitoring tools under ISO 27001
C. The organization must switch to real-time dashboards for all metrics without exception
B. Daily updates are excessive and create unnecessary compliance overhead
A. Real-time dashboards contradict the quarterly review schedule; data freshness must align with decision-making frequency
questions.justification_correct: Clause 9.1 requires monitoring and measurement to be timely and appropriate. Data freshness should support informed decision-making; quarterly reviews based on outdated data undermine effectiveness.
questions.justification_wrong: Dashboards and manual processes can coexist, but their update frequencies must be aligned to management decision-making cycles for relevance.

questions.text: During the management review, the CISO presents evidence that 95% of access control objectives have been met. The organization has not identified any nonconformities in this domain for 18 months. Per clause 9.3, what critical analysis is missing?
options: A. Analysis of effectiveness—whether controls actually prevent unauthorized access, not just compliance percentage achievement
D. Documentation that the CISO personally verified each control's operational effectiveness
C. Confirmation that the 95% metric exceeds the minimum 80% threshold required by ISO 27001
B. Verification that all nonconformities must be reported; absence of findings indicates perfect control design
questions.justification_correct: Clause 9.3 requires management review to consider effectiveness of controls and whether objectives are achieved. Compliance percentages without effectiveness assessment are insufficient.
questions.justification_wrong: No nonconformities doesn't mean controls work as intended; effectiveness requires active testing and evidence of risk mitigation.

questions.text: An internal audit identifies that the organization's audit schedule covers all information security domains but audits high-risk areas only once every 24 months. The audit plan does not differentiate by risk level. What does clause 9.2.1 require to correct this?
options: C. High-risk areas should be audited less frequently to avoid audit fatigue
A. Risk-based audit frequency: high-risk domains audited more frequently; audit plan must be based on organizational risk profile
D. Audit frequency is determined solely by external audit requirements; internal audits have no frequency requirement
B. All domains must be audited quarterly regardless of risk to ensure equal coverage
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.2.1 requires internal audits to be conducted at planned intervals based on risk. Audit frequency must be proportional to importance and risk level.
questions.justification_wrong: Equal audit frequency ignores risk differentiation; ISO 27001 explicitly requires risk-based audit planning.

questions.text: After management review, the organization identifies a need for continual improvement in incident response time. The improvement initiative is planned for implementation next fiscal year (12 months away). Per clause 10.3, what is the concern?
options: A. Clause 10.3 requires continual improvement to be ongoing; deferring implementation 12 months contradicts the principle of continuous progress
D. Continual improvement is optional if no nonconformities exist in incident response processes
B. Improvement initiatives must be completed before the next management review, typically within 6 months maximum
C. Scheduling improvements for next fiscal year is acceptable if documented in the budget plan
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.3 requires continual improvement to be ongoing. While timelines are contextual, a 12-month deferral without interim milestones contradicts the 'continual' requirement.
questions.justification_wrong: Deferring improvement contradicts continual improvement principle; organizations must establish progressive improvement schedules.

questions.text: During an internal audit of the ISMS, the auditor discovers that management review meetings are held quarterly, but documented evidence shows that nonconformities identified in previous reviews were not formally tracked or closed within defined timelines. According to ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.3, what is the primary deficiency?
options: A. The organization failed to establish and maintain documented procedures for handling nonconformities and implementing corrective actions
C. Management review must occur simultaneously with internal audits to ensure effectiveness
D. The auditor lacked sufficient competence to identify nonconformities during the review process
B. Quarterly review frequency is insufficient; monthly reviews are mandatory per standard requirements
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 10.1 requires documented procedures for nonconformity management, including identification, investigation, and corrective action implementation with defined timelines. Tracking closure is mandatory for ISMS improvement.
questions.justification_wrong: Review frequency depends on organizational context (Clause 9.3); simultaneous scheduling is not required; auditor competence is separate from nonconformity management procedures.

questions.text: An organization implements continuous monitoring of access logs and encryption effectiveness metrics. However, the ISMS documentation reveals that measurement results are collected but not formally analyzed against established baselines or performance indicators. Which clause requirement is not being met?
options: B. ISO 27001:2022 Clause 8.1 mandates real-time alert systems instead of baseline comparisons for all monitoring activities
C. Continuous monitoring eliminates the need for formal measurement procedures under ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.2
D. Performance indicators must be communicated to all employees monthly per ISO 27001:2022 compliance requirements
A. ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.1 requires analysis of measurement results to determine performance against objectives and identify improvement opportunities
questions.justification_correct: Clause 9.1 explicitly requires determining performance of the ISMS against objectives and KPIs through measurement and analysis. Raw data collection without analysis fails this requirement and prevents informed decision-making.
questions.justification_wrong: Real-time alerts are tools, not requirements; continuous monitoring does not eliminate formal procedures; communication frequency is organizational context-dependent, not prescriptive.

questions.text: During management review, the CISO presents statistical data showing that 15% of internal audit findings remain outstanding for over 180 days. Leadership requests clarification on whether this delays certification audits. What is the correct assessment per ISO 27001:2022 Clause 10.3?
options: C. Internal audit findings do not require corrective actions; only external audit findings mandate documented corrective measures
D. Certification bodies automatically grant extensions for organizations with more than 10% outstanding findings due to resource constraints
B. 180-day delays are acceptable for findings rated as low-risk under ISO 27001:2022 without documentation of risk justification
A. Outstanding nonconformities exceeding 180 days directly indicate ineffective corrective action processes and risk certification audit failure; closure timelines must be defined and monitored
questions.justification_correct: Clause 10.3 requires organizations to take action to deal with nonconformities, including corrective action with defined timelines. Prolonged outstanding items indicate ineffective corrective action implementation and undermine ISMS maturity.
questions.justification_wrong: Risk-based justification requires documented evidence; internal findings still require action; certification bodies do not grant automatic extensions for untimely closure.

questions.text: An organization conducts internal audits annually covering all control objectives. Auditors use a standardized checklist but do not document audit methodology, scope changes, or rationale for sampling decisions. The audit report states 'no nonconformities found.' According to ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.2, what is the primary audit deficiency?
options: A. Annual frequency is non-compliant; ISO 27001:2022 requires audits at least semi-annually for all organizations
B. The audit lacks documented audit program with defined criteria, scope, methodology, and evidence of auditor competence; undocumented sampling decisions compromise audit credibility
C. Standardized checklists are prohibited; auditors must create unique questionnaires for each audit cycle per ISO 27001:2022
D. Finding zero nonconformities indicates successful audit execution; additional documentation is unnecessary for certification purposes
questions.justification_correct: Clause 9.2 mandates documented audit programs defining frequency, methodology, scope, and selection criteria. Undocumented sampling decisions and missing audit plan documentation fail to demonstrate systematic, objective audit execution required for ISMS effectiveness.
questions.justification_wrong: Frequency depends on context (Clause 9.2); standardized tools are acceptable if documented; zero findings require evidence of audit effectiveness, not mere absence of documentation.

questions.text: During management review, metrics reveal that vulnerability remediation time increased from 30 days to 60 days, and two critical controls showed reduced effectiveness scores. Management decides to defer investigation pending budget approval for additional tools. How does this decision align with ISO 27001:2022 Clause 10.2?
options: A. Deferral is non-compliant; Clause 10.2 requires management to determine actions for improvement immediately upon identifying performance degradation and control ineffectiveness
C. Management review focuses only on strategic planning; operational metrics require separate technical committees, not management action
B. Budget constraints justify deferral under ISO 27001:2022; improvement actions are optional when resources are insufficient
D. Control effectiveness reduction is acceptable if vulnerability remediation remains below the industry average threshold
questions.justification_correct: Clause 10.2 requires management review to determine actions for continual improvement. Deferring investigation of degraded control effectiveness and increased remediation timelines violates the requirement for timely response to identified gaps.
questions.justification_wrong: Budget constraints do not override improvement requirements; management review directly addresses operational metrics; industry averages do not exempt organizations from addressing their own control degradation.

questions.text: During an internal audit of the ISMS, the auditor discovers that a critical control has not been operating as designed for the past three months. According to ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.2, what is the PRIMARY objective of this internal audit finding?
options: D. To replace the auditor conducting the audit
B. To identify all employees responsible for the control failure
C. To immediately terminate the non-conforming process
A. To determine conformity with ISO 27001 requirements and effectiveness of implemented controls
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.2 requires internal audits to determine whether the ISMS conforms to the organization's own requirements and to ISO 27001. Audits assess control effectiveness and identify opportunities for improvement.
questions.justification_wrong: While accountability is important, internal audits are not designed to assign blame. Immediate process termination or auditor replacement are not primary objectives of internal audit as per clause 9.2.

questions.text: An organization identifies that monitoring controls for user access have not been executed for six months. According to ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.1, what corrective action should be PRIORITIZED regarding the non-conformity?
options: A. Schedule the next monitoring activity and document lessons learned for future prevention
B. Immediately conduct retrospective access reviews and determine root cause to prevent recurrence
C. Dismiss the person responsible for monitoring oversight
D. Wait until the annual management review to address this issue
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.1 and 10.1 require organizations to monitor and measure controls. Non-conformities must be addressed through root cause analysis and corrective actions designed to prevent recurrence, not just future compliance.
questions.justification_wrong: Dismissing personnel avoids root cause analysis. Waiting for annual review delays necessary corrective action. Scheduling future monitoring without addressing the gap violates clause 10.2 corrective action requirements.

questions.text: During the management review (clause 9.3), the CISO presents data showing that 15 internal audit findings from the previous year remain open. According to ISO 27001:2022, what must the management review address?
options: B. Only the budget allocated for ISMS improvements in the next fiscal year
A. Whether corrective actions are effective and whether root causes have been adequately addressed
C. The names and departments of all staff involved in the audit findings
D. Whether external auditors should be replaced due to finding severity
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.3 requires management review to evaluate changes in internal and external context, including effectiveness of corrective actions and adequacy of resources. Open findings indicate potential root cause or effectiveness gaps.
questions.justification_wrong: While budget and staffing matter, clause 9.3 focuses on ISMS effectiveness and adequacy. Personnel identification and auditor replacement are not primary management review concerns per normative requirements.

questions.text: An organization discovers through monitoring that its patch management control has failed to remediate 12% of critical vulnerabilities within the defined timeframe. According to ISO 27001:2022 clause 10, what represents the MOST appropriate response framework?
options: B. Issue a warning to the IT team and schedule retraining immediately
C. Accept the 12% failure rate as an acceptable risk level and continue
A. Evaluate nonconformity severity, analyze root cause, implement corrective actions, evaluate effectiveness, and document results
D. Hire external contractors to manually patch all remaining systems within 48 hours
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.1 and 10.2 establish a comprehensive framework: evaluate nonconformities, determine and implement corrective actions addressing root causes, evaluate action effectiveness, and maintain records throughout.
questions.justification_wrong: Warning and retraining alone don't address root causes. Accepting failures contradicts clause 10 requirements. Emergency outsourcing bypasses systematic analysis required by clause 10.2 for sustainable correction.

questions.text: Following corrective actions implemented for a control nonconformity, how should the organization demonstrate EFFECTIVENESS per ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.2?
options: D. Through a one-time follow-up inspection conducted by senior management
C. By simply documenting that the corrective action was completed as planned
A. Through subsequent monitoring cycles, audit findings, metrics, and comparison against baseline to confirm the control now operates as designed
B. By obtaining written acknowledgment from the employee who caused the nonconformity
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.2 requires organizations to verify corrective action effectiveness through evaluation, which includes monitoring subsequent performance, audit results, metrics, and trend analysis to confirm sustained control operation.
questions.justification_wrong: Employee acknowledgment does not demonstrate control effectiveness. Completion documentation alone does not verify effectiveness. Single follow-up inspections lack the continuous monitoring required by clause 9.1 and 10.2.

questions.text: During an internal audit of the ISMS, you identify that management review meetings lack documented evidence of evaluation against the organization's information security objectives. According to ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.3, what is the primary risk of this non-conformity?
options: D. External auditors are prevented from conducting surveillance audits
C. The organization must immediately implement new security controls
A. The organization cannot demonstrate that the ISMS remains suitable, adequate, and effective over time
B. Internal auditors will lose their certification credentials
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.3 requires management review to evaluate ISMS suitability and effectiveness. Without documented evidence linking review outcomes to objectives achievement, the organization cannot demonstrate continuous alignment with strategic goals and regulatory requirements.
questions.justification_wrong: Options B, C, D misrepresent audit consequences, control implementation triggers, and external audit procedures respectively. They don't address the core requirement for documented evaluation of ISMS effectiveness.

questions.text: An organization discovers through monitoring (clause 9.1) that 15% of its security incidents were not reported within the required timeframe. The incident response procedure is already documented and approved. What corrective action approach aligns best with ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.2?
options: D. Replace the incident response procedure with a completely new one
A. Immediately terminate all employees involved in late incident reporting
B. Analyze root causes (awareness gaps, process barriers, system delays) and implement targeted improvements addressing identified causes
C. Increase the incident reporting timeframe to match current performance
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.2 requires corrective actions to address root causes of non-conformities, not symptoms. Root cause analysis (awareness, procedures, tools) followed by targeted improvements ensures sustainable compliance and prevents recurrence.
questions.justification_wrong: Option A is disproportionate. Option C masks the problem. Option D ignores RCA methodology. Only Option B follows clause 10.2's requirement for root cause-driven corrective actions leading to systemic improvements.

questions.text: During internal audit planning (clause 9.2), you must determine the audit scope and frequency for multiple departments. Which approach best reflects the risk-based methodology required by ISO 27001:2022?
options: A. Audit all departments equally every 6 months regardless of their information security risk profile
B. Conduct all audits in the same month to reduce administrative burden
C. Prioritize audit frequency and scope based on assessed risk, criticality of information assets, and prior non-conformities in each department
D. Audit only departments with documented incidents from the previous year
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.2 requires risk-based internal audit planning. Scope and frequency must reflect each area's risk profile, asset criticality, and compliance history. This ensures audit resources address highest-risk areas proportionately.
questions.justification_wrong: Option A ignores risk differentiation. Option B prioritizes convenience over risk. Option D is reactive, only addressing past incidents. Only Option C implements the normative requirement for risk-based, targeted audit planning.

questions.text: An organization's ISMS monitoring data (clause 9.1) reveals that 8 of 20 implemented controls are not achieving their stated objectives. Management requests postponing remediation for budget reasons. How should the Lead Implementer advise proceeding per ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.1 and 10.1?
options: D. Remove the 8 ineffective controls from the ISMS documentation
B. Immediately declare the organization non-compliant with ISO 27001:2022
A. Accept the postponement and document it as a management decision
C. Record the control ineffectiveness as non-conformities, assess risk implications, and establish urgent remediation timelines with management commitment
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clauses 9.1 and 10.1 require organizations to monitor control effectiveness and address non-conformities promptly. Documenting control failures as non-conformities, assessing residual risk, and establishing remediation timelines with management buy-in maintains ISMS integrity and auditability.
questions.justification_wrong: Option A permits uncontrolled risk deferral. Option B is premature without remediation attempts. Option D removes accountability. Option C balances practical remediation with risk management and documented decision-making.

questions.text: During management review (clause 9.3), you present data showing that security awareness training completion rates declined from 92% to 74%. The organization's risk appetite for awareness gaps is low. Which analysis and recommendation best reflects ISO 27001:2022 requirements for continual improvement (clause 10.3)?
options: A. Recommend ignoring the trend as temporary fluctuation and monitoring for one more year
D. Accept the decline and adjust risk assessment to accommodate lower awareness levels
C. Immediately update the information security policy to lower awareness training requirements
B. Analyze root causes (scheduling conflicts, accessibility issues, engagement), implement targeted improvements, adjust training delivery, and establish preventive measures to restore compliance
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.3 requires continual improvement through analysis of performance trends, identification of improvement opportunities, and implementation of preventive/corrective measures. Root cause analysis and targeted interventions address the decline while maintaining control effectiveness.
questions.justification_wrong: Option A ignores adverse trends. Option C lowers standards inappropriately. Option D accepts elevated risk when appetite is low. Option B aligns with clause 10.3's requirement for data-driven, proactive improvement initiatives addressing identified performance gaps.

questions.text: An organization conducts internal audits semi-annually and identifies a critical non-conformity in access control procedures. According to ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.2, what is the primary objective of the internal audit program regarding this finding?
options: D. To replace the need for management review activities
C. To ensure external auditors have minimal findings during certification audits
B. To identify all possible vulnerabilities across the entire IT infrastructure
A. To determine conformity with ISO 27001 requirements and effectiveness of the ISMS implementation
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.2.1 establishes that internal audits shall determine whether the ISMS conforms to requirements and is effectively implemented. This directly supports the audit objective regarding non-conformities.
questions.justification_wrong: While vulnerability identification is important, internal audits per ISO 27001 specifically focus on conformity and effectiveness of the ISMS, not comprehensive security vulnerability assessments. External audit preparation is a byproduct, not primary objective.

questions.text: During management review per ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.3, the CISO presents metrics showing a 15% increase in detected security incidents. Which input is MANDATORY for the management review process according to normative requirements?
options: B. Competitive intelligence and benchmarking data from industry peers only
A. Results of internal audits, non-conformities, corrective actions, and changes in external/internal issues
C. Individual employee performance ratings and disciplinary actions
D. Vendor and third-party security certifications exclusively
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.3.2 explicitly requires management review inputs including internal audit results, non-conformity status, corrective action effectiveness, and changing context. These are mandatory inputs for comprehensive review.
questions.justification_wrong: Competitive benchmarking, individual performance metrics, and vendor certifications, while potentially useful, are not mandatory inputs specified in ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.3.2 for management review.

questions.text: An organization discovers a non-conformity where encryption key management procedures were not followed in 12 instances. According to ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.1, what is the required response timeframe for initiating corrective actions?
options: D. Within 90 days maximum to allow comprehensive root cause analysis
B. As soon as possible and within a timeframe determined by the organization based on risk and urgency
A. Within 30 days of detection, without exception
C. Only after the next scheduled management review meeting
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.1.1 requires organizations to take action to deal with non-conformities 'as soon as possible' and within a timeframe 'determined by the organization based on the significance and risk involved.' This allows risk-based flexibility.
questions.justification_wrong: Fixed timeframes (30, 90 days) contradict ISO 27001's risk-based approach. Management review linkage is indirect. ISO 27001 emphasizes organizational determination based on significance and risk, not absolute deadlines.

questions.text: When evaluating the effectiveness of corrective actions per ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.1, an organization must assess whether the root cause has been eliminated. Which technique is MOST appropriate for determining root cause in a CISO's incident involving unauthorized privileged access?
questions.tag: Root Cause Analysis and Corrective Action Effectiveness
options: B. Immediate suspension of all user accounts to prevent recurrence
D. Statistical sampling of 5% of all access logs for the past month
A. Five Why's analysis combined with control flow mapping and forensic log review
C. Review of competitor security policies to identify better practices
questions.justification_correct: Root cause analysis requires systematic investigation. Five Why's methodology, control flow mapping, and forensic evidence (logs) together provide comprehensive causal determination, enabling targeted corrective actions per ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.1.2.
questions.justification_wrong: Reactive account suspension doesn't address root cause. Competitor benchmarking lacks organizational context. Statistical sampling (5%) is insufficient for incident-specific root cause determination; forensic completeness is needed.

questions.text: An organization's ISMS has achieved conformity with ISO 27001:2022, but management review reveals declining effectiveness metrics over two consecutive reviews. According to clause 10.3, what must the organization prioritize for continual improvement?
options: D. Reduce audit frequency to decrease detection of performance issues
B. Identify improvement opportunities using monitoring data, audit results, and analysis of non-conformity trends
C. Procure advanced security tools without assessment to artificially improve metrics
A. Immediately re-baseline all KPIs and reset targets to reduce performance pressure on teams
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.3 requires continual improvement through monitoring data analysis, internal audit results, management review outputs, and non-conformity trends. This evidence-based approach identifies genuine improvement opportunities addressing root causes.
questions.justification_wrong: Arbitrary KPI resets mask problems. Tool procurement without assessment wastes resources. Reducing audit frequency contradicts ISO 27001's monitoring requirements and prevents visibility into declining effectiveness, violating clause 10.3 principles.

questions.text: An organization conducting internal audits discovers that control A5.23 (Information security for supplier relationships) is not operating effectively. The audit evidence shows suppliers lack contractual security obligations. Per ISO 27001:2022 clause 9.2, what is the primary auditor responsibility?
options: A. Report findings and recommend corrective actions; auditor documents non-conformity with root cause analysis for management review
B. Immediately implement supplier security training without awaiting management response
C. Terminate supplier relationships pending corrective action completion
D. Advise suppliers directly to amend contracts without organization notification
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 9.2 requires auditors to report findings objectively. Non-conformities must be documented with evidence. Management reviews findings (9.3) to determine corrective actions. Auditor role is advisory, not implementation authority.
questions.justification_wrong: Auditors lack implementation authority (9.2). Direct supplier engagement bypasses organizational governance. Immediate termination exceeds auditor scope—corrective actions are management responsibility per 10.1.

questions.text: During management review (clause 9.3), the organization identifies that monitoring metrics for A6.8 (Access control) show 15% of user accounts lack periodic re-certification. The ISMS manager proposes postponing corrective action for 6 months due to resource constraints. What should be the review team's decision per ISO 27001:2022?
options: B. Require immediate interim controls and establish specific timeline for full remediation; document risk acceptance if postponement approved
C. Delegate decision entirely to the ISMS manager without documented justification
D. Immediately terminate all non-certified accounts regardless of business impact
A. Approve postponement; resource constraints justify delayed remediation of control gaps
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 9.3 requires management review of audit findings and performance data. Organizations must determine if non-conformities pose unacceptable risks. Postponement requires documented risk acceptance and interim mitigation per A5.1 (control selection).
questions.justification_wrong: Unconditional approval ignores risk management responsibility. Delegation without documented rationale violates clause 9.3 review requirements. Blanket termination ignores proportionality and may violate A8.1 user authentication principles.

questions.text: An organization's monitoring process (9.1) reveals that incident response time for severity-1 incidents averages 4 hours against a 2-hour KPI. Root cause analysis shows inadequate on-call staffing. When planning corrective action per clause 10.1, which approach aligns with ISO 27001:2022 principles?
options: A. Adjust KPI to 4 hours to match current performance; no staffing changes required
B. Identify root cause (staffing), implement staffing plan, redefine escalation procedures, monitor effectiveness over 30 days, document lessons learned
D. Outsource all incident response to third party; eliminate internal monitoring
C. Increase monitoring frequency from monthly to weekly without addressing staffing
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 10.1 mandates corrective actions address root causes, not symptoms. Effective corrective action: identify cause (staffing gap), implement solution (resource plan), define monitoring metrics, verify effectiveness. Documentation supports continual improvement (9.3, 10.3).
questions.justification_wrong: Adjusting KPI without remediation masks underlying control failure (10.1 intent). Monitoring frequency increase without root cause resolution is ineffective. Outsourcing transfers rather than corrects the problem; contradicts control ownership.

questions.text: Following internal audit, the organization identifies 8 minor non-conformities related to A5.15 (Access control decision-making). Management determines these do not require immediate corrective action but merit process improvements. Per ISO 27001:2022 clause 10.2, how should continual improvement be documented?
options: C. Archive audit findings without further action; continual improvement requires only major non-conformities
A. Record improvements informally in team emails; formal documentation unnecessary for minor issues
D. Implement improvements immediately without planning or risk assessment
B. Document improvement initiatives with objectives, responsibilities, timelines, resource allocation, and post-implementation effectiveness reviews
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 10.2 requires organizations to determine needs/opportunities for improvement, implement improvements, and evaluate effectiveness. Clause 8.6 mandates documented improvement processes. All improvements—major or minor—require systematic planning and verification.
questions.justification_wrong: Informal documentation violates clause 8.6 (documented information). Excluding minor items contradicts 10.2 continual improvement scope. Archiving without action abandons improvement opportunity. Unplanned implementation risks unintended consequences and effectiveness gaps.

questions.text: An organization's ISMS records show corrective actions from three consecutive audits addressing the same control (A6.2 user registration/de-registration). The fourth audit identifies the same non-conformity recurrence. Per ISO 27001:2022 clauses 9.2, 10.1, and 10.3, what systemic failure should management address?
questions.tag: Effectiveness of Corrective Actions and Root Cause Analysis
options: B. Accept recurring issues as inevitable; increase audit frequency as compensating control
A. Previous corrective actions addressed symptoms only; conduct deeper root cause analysis examining procedural, technical, and cultural factors; verify corrective action effectiveness before closure
C. Replace the ISMS manager; personnel changes resolve systemic control failures
D. Discontinue internal audits of this control; audit frequency may be causing the non-conformity
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 10.1 requires corrective actions address root causes, not just symptoms. Recurrence across four audit cycles indicates ineffective previous actions. Clause 10.3 demands verification of effectiveness. Deep-root analysis (5-Why or fishbone) and post-implementation monitoring are mandatory before closure.
questions.justification_wrong: Accepting recurrence violates continual improvement principle (10.3). Personnel replacement ignores systemic process failures. Audit reduction eliminates detection mechanism—does not correct underlying control deficiency. Previous corrective actions failed verification; more audits would correctly expose the gap.
